comments.body:
Hello Team,

Good Day!

The address correction is a suggestion from the plugin. If you are certain that the address provided is correct, you can ignore the suggestion and proceed as usual.

Additionally, upon checking, I could observe that you have provided the Country of Origin as Canada in the app, however, the country code CA is needed instead of Canada.

I have updated the products in the app which had the Country of Origin as Canada to CA.

You can reprocess the orders in the app and try generating the label.

For your reference, to reprocess Orders that are in **Initial/Processing** Status in the app, you can select the orders and use the **Reprocess Orders** option.
For Orders that are in **Label Failed** Status in the app, you can select the order -> More options -> Change Status to Initial. Once the order is in Initial Status, follow the above process.

Let us know if you face any other difficulties.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Team,
 
Good Day!
 
The address correction is a suggestion from the plugin. If you are certain that the address provided is correct, you can ignore the suggestion and proceed as usual.
 
Additionally, upon checking, I could observe that you have provided the Country of Origin as Canada in the app, however, the country code CA is needed instead of Canada.
 
I have updated the products in the app which had the Country of Origin as Canada to CA.
 
You can reprocess the orders in the app and try generating the label. 
 
For your reference, to reprocess Orders that are in Initial/Processing Status in the app, you can select the orders and use the Reprocess Orders option.
For Orders that are in Label Failed Status in the app, you can select the order -> More options -> Change Status to Initial. Once the order is in Initial Status, follow the above process.
 
Let us know if you face any other difficulties.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Victoria,

The products you shared that were not imported into the plugin are **composite products**. Currently, the plugin supports **simple and variable products** but does not support composite products. This is why the respective orders were not imported, and shipping labels could not be generated for them.

If I understand correctly, a **composite product** is a type of product that allows multiple simple or variable products to be combined into a single offering, often used for customizable bundles or kits.

Since customers are selecting options within the composite product, which are actually **simple or variable products**, would it be possible to make orders to create **without using the composite product**, and instead create the order with only the selected variants?

With the current setup, the plugin does not support composite products, so any orders placed with composite products need to be processed **outside the plugin**. Or as workaround, you need to edit thes orders within WooCommerce and remove composite product manually so plugin will import order and allow generating labels

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any further assistance — we’re happy to help.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/V3E98of1xHIdvuRMhqIwXNuts/?name=image.png)

Hi Natasha,



The price part is considered a variation still? A gift box is a set starting price, but if they select a flavour that is a bit more expensive there is a slight upcharge added onto the final price of the gift box. That is considered a variation? But not visible to customers, right?



We worked through the upcharges on Create Your Own boxes a few months back. This is our upcharges allotted per 250ml flavour: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1i_HU269eyKiUeqVO1bIgp_t5w_1Qq-2Dy_QjUqwtTbI/edit?usp=sharing



To note, the 12 x 250ml gift box has the most variations. Our variations go from the least complicated (2 variations) up to the most complex (12 variations).

Hi Victoria,

I just looked at https://greavesjams.com/product/connoisseur-jam-collection-create-your-own-12-x-250ml/

Yes, anything different in the dropdown menu is considered a variation, the attached screengrab shows the variation of flavour and also price. 

Do you want us to start with the 12 x 250 to inform the estimate of rebuild?

We can use your current products for reference to the rebuild - however should there be any requirements outside of what is visible on the frontend please let me know. 

Thank you,
Natasha




On Sep 30, 2025, at 11:41 AM, Greaves Jams <greaves@greavesjams.com> wrote:

Hi Natasha,
 
The price part is considered a variation still? A gift box is a set starting price, but if they select a flavour that is a bit more expensive there is a slight upcharge added onto the final price of the gift box. That is considered a variation? But not visible to customers, right?
 
We worked through the upcharges on Create Your Own boxes a few months back. This is our upcharges allotted per 250ml flavour: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1i_HU269eyKiUeqVO1bIgp_t5w_1Qq-2Dy_QjUqwtTbI/edit?usp=sharing
 
To note, the 12 x 250ml gift box has the most variations. Our variations go from the least complicated (2 variations) up to the most complex (12 variations).
 
Ideally, we want these products to be built as easy as possible, so they are supported in most plugins. Avoiding custom code if that causes pains in the future.
 
Thanks,
 
<image003.jpg>
Victoria Groetelaars
Greaves Jams & Marmalades, Box 26, Niagara on the Lake, ON, Canada L0S 1J0
www.greavesjams.com  1-905-468-3608  1-800-515-9939

Thanks Victoria,
 
We will need to test on DEV first to inform the time to rebuild these products. We can start with the 6x250 and provide you with a quote, hope that works for you!
 
I just looked up the rebuild on Woocommerce - variable products can include different prices, however we need to look into if they can also include flavour + price variations (2 variations). 
 
We can also look into pulling the actual products into a variable product vs via custom code (because all of these products already exist on the site).
 
Thank you!
Natasha
 


On Sep 30, 2025, at 11:10 AM, Greaves Jams <greaves@greavesjams.com> wrote:
 
Hi Natasha,
 
Angela can certainly enlighten you with a call. Spencer built these products years ago when we moved from OS Commerce to WooCommerce around 2017ish. 
 
Can a variable product allow customers to select the flavour options in one box? They need to, for example, select 6 flavours within one 6 x 250ml gift box product. I believe all the products with this custom code are (but perhaps you want to verify by pulling the product data?):
  • 2 x 250ml Deluxe Gift Box –Create your Own–
  • 3 x 250ml Citrine & Navy Gift Box — Create Your Own —
  • 4 x 250ml Premium Gift Box –Create Your Own–
  • 6 x 250ml Canadian Tradition Gift Box– “Create Your Own” —
  • 12 x 250ml Connoisseur Collection - Create Your Own - 
 
The above products grab the pricing and flavour information from hidden product called “Greaves 250ml” with sku 2500-CUSTOM. This product is only visible in the backend to connect to those Create Your Own boxes for flavour selection. I only go in the backend here to update when we are out of stock on certain flavours, or if there is a change in the upcharge for certain flavours. 
 
If we start from scratch now, how can these be built to work the way we need? What is the time estimate to build this? 
 
Thanks,
 
<image003.jpg>
Victoria Groetelaars
Greaves Jams & Marmalades, Box 26, Niagara on the Lake, ON, Canada L0S 1J0
www.greavesjams.com  1-905-468-3608  1-800-515-9939

Cc: FERNANDO <fernando@kaiser.ca>; PluginHive <support@pluginhive.com>
Subject: Re: [PluginHive] My order is asking for an address correction but i...
 
Good Morning Victoria,
 
Fernando will need to rebuild these as “variable” products, as pluginhive does not support the current build of the composite products (for example, your products with different prices in the Create your own box shown).
 
To note, Fernando can pull the list of composite products with the variables within - alternatively if you provide us with a list of all composite product, including the variations within for reference, that will allow us to build more efficiently. 
 
Also, if you happen to know why the products were originally built as composite vs variable, please let us know so that we can look into what exactly needs to be rebuilt.
 
Once we rebuild, we may also need to create redirects to avoid 404 broken links. 
 
Thanks so much!
Natasha

Canada Customer using the WSS plugin with USP, Canpar and Canada Post for rates and labels.
Customer had reported that some of the orders are not importing in the plugin as the associated products are not imported in the plugin.
Below are the products that are not imported in the plugin -

- 4 x 250ml Premium Gift Box –Create Your Own–

- 12 x 250ml Connoisseur Collection - Create Your Own -

Hi Manohar, 

Thank you for confirming that composite products are not supported at this time. 

We need to find an alternative solution for this type of product, can you please confirm if any of these plugins/ products types are supported by pluginhive (we don’t want to build anything out further until we get confirmation it will integrate well with pluginhive. 

We have found the following options online, however need to confirm if Pluginhive will support any of these options:

    • Alternative: Advanced Product Fields for WooCommerce (by Studio Wombat) – free & flexible.


Thanks very much,
Natasha

Hello Victoria,
 
The products you shared that were not imported into the plugin are composite products. Currently, the plugin supports simple and variable products but does not support composite products. This is why the respective orders were not imported, and shipping labels could not be generated for them.
 
If I understand correctly, a composite product is a type of product that allows multiple simple or variable products to be combined into a single offering, often used for customizable bundles or kits.
 
Since customers are selecting options within the composite product, which are actually simple or variable products, would it be possible to make orders to create without using the composite product, and instead create the order with only the selected variants?
 
With the current setup, the plugin does not support composite products, so any orders placed with composite products need to be processed outside the plugin. Or as workaround, you need to edit thes orders within WooCommerce and remove composite product manually so plugin will import order and allow generating labels
 
Please let us know how it goes or if you need any further assistance — we’re happy to help.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

For Orders that are in Label Failed Status in the app, you can select the order -> More options -> Change Status to Initial. Once the order is in Initial Status, follow the above process.
 
Let us know if you face any other difficulties.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
